text.text:
Saw this restaurant being hyped up on social media and decided to give it a try as an early Thanksgiving dinner with my brother. Although the price point is kind of high, the food did not disappoint.

We called a day ahead and ordered the 4hr Clay Pot Roasted Duck. The duck meat was very moist and packed with flavor. The rice, lentils, and sweet potatoes stuffed into the duck were delicious as well. Most definitely lived up to the hype.

Before the duck arrived we also ordered some of the Sliced Duck and Pork Belly. Both were amazing, but the Pork Belly was the star of the show. Some of the best I've ever enjoyed. We even asked for a second order at the end of our meal.

Service here was pretty standard for a Korean restaurant. Since it's not an AYCE spot, the server cooked the meats for us and served the Clay Pot Roasted Duck for us. The interior is pretty classic for a Korean restaurant.

Overall, everything was amazing and worth the price. Not sure if I'd make the drive to splurge again unless it's a special occasion.

text.text:
We preordered a signature roasted duck for our party of three. Roasted duck is stuffed with purple rice, some herbs, pumpkin seeds. Very nutritious and delicious. The texture tastes like chicken at the restaurant, so fresh and tender! Highly recommend dine in for the duck to get the freshest taste. I would definitely come back to try duck bbq
p.s. I took some leftovers home and microwave the next day. It became regular duck taste.

text.text:
My family was driving back from Ojai and searched for Korean food along the way. This restaurant came up with good reviews, so we decided to give it a try.
The restaurant is located in an older shopping area, but the place itself was very clean. We arrived around 3:30 PM, so it wasn’t busy at all. We were seated right away, and the staff were incredibly friendly and welcoming.
There were four of us, and we ordered just one Smoked Duck platter. The portion was huge and more than enough for everyone. It was $125 before tax and came to about $137 total for four people.
The meal included a lot of banchan, as shown in the photos, plus a bowl of rice for each person. The duck was lightly pan-seared and perfectly seasoned—not too salty. Wrapped with the pink pickled radish, it was absolutely delicious. It also paired really well with the rice and banchan.
After we finished the duck, the staff took away the pan and brought out a spicy kimchi noodle soup, which was also very tasty. The best part was that everything was included in the original order, there were no extra charges.
We had a great experience and will definitely come back to try their Roasted Duck next time. It seems to be the restaurant’s signature dish since it needs to be ordered in advance. Highly recommended! ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

text.text:
Came here because a friend raved out some roasted duck.  Called in 3-4 hours in advance and was immediately served once we had arrived with our reso.

Between the 3 of us we shared one order of duck and felt it was a sufficient amount of food. The herbs and seeds that they used to cook the duck in was very unique. The sweet potato inside was absolutely delicious.

Service was very strong as they did cut the duck up table side. Overall felt it was just a good experience but not life changing given the cost of the meal being $120+ for one dish.

text.text: Wanted to try this place for a while. We went before the dinner rush, so it was nice and quiet. The building and decor is a bit old. They have a parking lot behind, but I didn’t see where the access was. I parked at the Koreatown Galleria since it’s free and covered. It was just a short walk to the restaurant. The texture of the duck meat goes really well with the spicy dipping sauce. We ordered the smoked duck, which was good for two of us. Although I was still a little hungry even with the summer noodles. The smoked duck had more of a subtle flavor. The summer noodles were very plain. I wouldn’t mind going again, but I’d order the sliced duck and maybe the kimchi fried rice.
